proposals.title: UP_TRI × Bear: n=97, 93.7% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=UP_TRI, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +42.3pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.8690 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.61 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
approval would propagate overlap to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=97, wr=93.7%, edge +42.3pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification remains robust (n=97, Wilson lower 0.869, +42.3pp edge over UP_TRI baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.61 below the Tier S threshold. Cohort is regime-gated to Bear while current regime is Bull (shifting, day 3), so the cohort is dormant and the flagged 94.92% Bull concentration on 59 open positions is not compounded by a Bear-gated promotion. Consistent with continuous prior approvals (2026-04-29 through 2026-07-02) and no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: BULL_PROXY × Bear: n=17, 88.2% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=BULL_PROXY, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +29.9pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.6566 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.98 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
R-multiple 0.98 remains just below the Tier S threshold and is subject to D-8 review of Day-6 forced-exit dominance; if r_multiple drifts below 0.9, auto-demotion should fire per §3.
approval would propagate redundant to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=17, wr=88.2%, edge +29.9pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification remains robust (n=17, Wilson lower 0.6566 above Tier M floor, +29.9pp edge over BULL_PROXY baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.98 (<1.0). Cohort is regime-gated to Bear while current regime is Bull (shifting, day 3), so the cohort is dormant; portfolio Bear exposure is 0 and the flagged 94.92% concentration is on Bull, so a Bear-gated BULL_PROXY promotion does not compound the concentration risk and may diversify when Bear re-emerges. Consistent with continuous prior approvals of this exact cohort (2026-06-19 through 07-02) with no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: Exposure concentration: by_regime + by_signal_type + by_signal_type×regime×sector = Bull regime 94.92%; UP_TRI 98.31%; UP_TRI×Bull×Bank 20.34% (94.9%)

claim.what: Concentration risk: by_regime + by_signal_type + by_signal_type×regime×sector = Bull regime 94.92%; UP_TRI 98.31%; UP_TRI×Bull×Bank 20.34%; recommended action: Pause new entries in signal_type=UP_TRI AND sector=Bank until Bank cell drops below 0.20 via natural Day-6 exits; additionally pause new UP_TRI entries in regime=Bull until Bull×UP_TRI cohort promotes above Candidate tier or regime concentration falls below 0.80. Consider index-level short hedge (e.g., Nifty/BankNifty put spread) sized to ~20-30% of Bank exposure to cap tail risk on a Bull→Choppy/Bear flip.
claim.expected_effect: Trader awareness of tail risk: A Bull→Bear or Bull→Choppy regime flip would simultaneously invalidate 56 of 59 positions with no offsetting shorts and no validated cohort edge to lean on. The Bank cluster (12 positions) would gap-correlate on any rate-shock or credit-stress catalyst, and the UP_TRI monoculture means all exits trigger on the same trend-break signal — the book unwinds as a single trade.

evidence: regime_concentration.triggered=true: Bull observed 0.9492 vs threshold 0.80 (56 of 59 open)
UP_TRI signal-type share 58/59 = 0.9831, above 0.85 threshold — book is a near-monoculture directional long
UP_TRI×Bull×Bank cell at 12/59 = 0.2034, breaches 0.20 cell threshold
Adjacent cells stack same-direction risk: Energy 0.1525, Infra 0.1356, Other 0.1356 — all UP_TRI×Bull
All four concentrated cells match Bull×UP_TRI cohort at Candidate tier (n=4) — unvalidated edge
Only 3 non-Bull positions (Choppy) provide no meaningful diversification against a regime flip
risks: Recommendation may be over-cautious; concentration may be edge-validated
Trader may already be aware; informational only

proposals.reversibility: Informational warning; no system mutation. Suppresses on next run if concentration drops below threshold.

decision_metadata.recommended_action: Pause new entries in signal_type=UP_TRI AND sector=Bank until Bank cell drops below 0.20 via natural Day-6 exits; additionally pause new UP_TRI entries in regime=Bull until Bull×UP_TRI cohort promotes above Candidate tier or regime concentration falls below 0.80. Consider index-level short hedge (e.g., Nifty/BankNifty put spread) sized to ~20-30% of Bank exposure to cap tail risk on a Bull→Choppy/Bear flip.
decision_metadata.tail_risk_scenario: A Bull→Bear or Bull→Choppy regime flip would simultaneously invalidate 56 of 59 positions with no offsetting shorts and no validated cohort edge to lean on. The Bank cluster (12 positions) would gap-correlate on any rate-shock or credit-stress catalyst, and the UP_TRI monoculture means all exits trigger on the same trend-break signal — the book unwinds as a single trade.
decision_metadata.llm_rationale: Regime concentration is triggered at 0.9492 (Bull, well above the 0.80 threshold) AND the UP_TRI×Bull×Bank cell breaches the 0.20 cell threshold at 20.34% (12 of 59 positions). UP_TRI also dominates signal mix at 58/59 = 98.3% (above 0.85 threshold), making the book a near-monoculture directional long. Matched Bull×UP_TRI cohort sits only at Candidate tier (n=4) — unvalidated edge across all four concentrated cells (Bank 20.34%, Energy 15.25%, Infra 13.56%, Other 13.56%).
